info.name: Tirumala Backend API
info.description:
API collection for the Tirumala TTD backend service.

Set the `baseUrl` variable to your running server (default: `http://localhost:3000`).

## Authentication
Write endpoints (POST / PUT / PATCH / DELETE) require a JWT Bearer token.

**Steps:**
1. Call **Auth → Login** with your username + password.
2. The token is automatically saved to the `authToken` collection variable.
3. All subsequent write requests will include `Authorization: Bearer {{authToken}}` automatically.

## Routes
- `/health` — server health check
- `/api/auth` — login
- `/api/darshan` — TTD pilgrim stats
- `/api/ssd` — SSD Token / Free Tickets status
- `/api/places` — places & regions
- `/api/services` — services catalog
- `/api/wallpapers` — wallpapers
- `/api/help` — FAQs, dress code, dos & don'ts, contacts
- `/api/ssd-locations` — SSD pickup locations
- `/api/scraper` — manual scrape triggers

variable.description: JWT token returned by POST /api/auth/login — auto-populated by the Login request test script.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Obtain a JWT token. The Login request test script auto-saves the token to the `authToken` collection variable.

exec: console.log('Token saved to authToken variable:', json.token.substring(0, 20) + '...');

request.description:
Authenticate with username + password.

On success the test script automatically stores the JWT in the `authToken` collection variable so all subsequent write requests are authenticated.

Token is valid for the duration set in `JWT_EXPIRES_IN` (default 7 days).
Returns server status, service name, timestamp and current environment.

Returns paginated darshan stats from Supabase.

**Query params:**
- `page` (default: 1)
- `limit` (default: 10)
Returns all records from Supabase without pagination. Useful for seeding or full data export.

Upserts a darshan update record (conflicts on `date`).

Simultaneously:
1. Persists to **Supabase** `darshan_updates` table
2. Pushes live pilgrim data to **Firebase RTDB** so all connected app clients update instantly

**Body fields:**
- `date` â€” YYYY-MM-DD format (unique key)
- `pilgrims` â€” formatted pilgrim count string
- `tonsures` â€” formatted tonsure count
- `hundi` â€” formatted hundi collection amount
- `waiting` â€” sarva darshan waiting time (e.g. "12H")
- `darshan_time` â€” seva darshan wait time (e.g. "6H")

Returns the current state of the background TTD website poller.

**Fields:**
- `isRunning` â€” whether a scrape cycle is in progress
- `lastRunAt` â€” ISO timestamp of last run
- `lastError` â€” last error message (null if healthy)
- `totalRuns` â€” total cycles since server start
- `totalUpdates` â€” cycles that detected changes and wrote to Firebase

Manually trigger one full scrape-compare-update cycle immediately, outside the cron schedule.

If data has changed since the last run, Firebase and Supabase are updated.

**Response fields:**
- `updated` â€” true if any data changed and was written
- `changes` â€” list of human-readable change descriptions
- `elapsed_ms` â€” how long the scrape took
- `errors` â€” non-fatal warnings from the scraper
- `raw` â€” the full raw scrape result

response.name: 200 OK â€” data changed

request.description:
Runs the Playwright scraper and returns raw parsed data **without writing anything** to Firebase or Supabase.

Use this to:
- Debug what the scraper currently sees on the TTD website
- Test selector changes
- Verify parsing logic without side effects

response.name: 200 OK

item.name: Scrape SSD Live from tirumala.org â­

request.description:
Scrapes the **Slotted Sarva Darshan** block from the TIRUMALA UPDATES section of https://www.tirumala.org/ in real time using a Playwright headless Chromium browser.

Source text on the website:
```
Running Slot: 1 on 27-Feb-2026
Balance tickets for 27-Feb-2026: 0
Note: Live tickets status may vary...
```

**No database reads or writes** â€” this is a pure live scrape from TTD's website.

**Response fields:**
- `running_slot` â€” current slot number/code being called
- `slot_date` â€” ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD)
- `slot_date_display` â€” original TTD format (27-Feb-2026)
- `balance_tickets` â€” remaining tickets available
- `balance_date` / `balance_date_display` â€” date the balance applies to
- `note` â€” full note text from TTD
- `scraped_at` â€” ISO timestamp of when this was scraped

**Note:** Takes ~5-10 seconds as Playwright loads a real browser.

response.name: 200 OK â€” scraped successfully
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "data": {
    "running_slot": "1",
    "slot_date": "2026-02-27",
    "slot_date_display": "27-Feb-2026",
    "balance_tickets": "0",
    "balance_date": "2026-02-27",
    "balance_date_display": "27-Feb-2026",
    "note": "Live tickets status may vary by the time pilgrim reaches physically at the counters. Pilgrims are waiting in the Q-line to take the SSD tokens. Tickets will be issued on first-come-first-service basis",
    "raw_text": "Running Slot: 1 on 27-Feb-2026 Balance tickets for 27-Feb-2026: 0 Note: ...",
    "scraped_at": "2026-02-27T05:30:00.000Z"
  }
}

response.name: 502 â€” scrape failed

request.description:
Returns the most recent SSD Token status from **Supabase** (persistent storage).

Use this for historical data or when Firebase is unavailable.

Returns live SSD Token status directly from **Firebase RTDB**.

This is the fastest and most up-to-date source â€” used by the mobile app's real-time listener.

Update SSD Token status. Simultaneously:
1. Persists to **Supabase** `ssd_status` table (conflicts on `date`)
2. Pushes live update to **Firebase RTDB** so all app clients update instantly

**Body fields:**
- `running_slot` â€” current token being called (e.g. "C-380")
- `balance_tickets` â€” remaining available tickets (e.g. "1,240")
- `date` â€” YYYY-MM-DD format (unique key)
Returns the current Sarva Darshan queue wait time text from Firebase RTDB.

Lightweight PATCH to update **only** the Sarva Darshan queue waiting time text in Firebase RTDB.

Does not write to Supabase â€” Firebase-only update for real-time display in the app.

**Body fields:**
- `queue_time` â€” wait time string displayed in the app (e.g. "18H", "10H 30M", "SOLD OUT")
Returns grouped service categories and services from Supabase `services_catalog`.
Returns one service detail by id, including listing icon image (`iconImage`) and detail gallery images (`images[]`).

request.description:
Uploads a single icon image for listing cards and stores in `services_catalog.icon_image` + `icon_image_public_id`.

Form-data:
- `image`: file

Uploads one gallery image for service detail page and inserts into `service_images` with sort order.

Form-data:
- `image`: file
Deletes one detail gallery image from Cloudinary and `service_images` table.
Creates one new service item in `services_catalog`.
Updates one service row by id.

Sets or clears `booking_date` (ISO-8601 timestamptz) and/or `instructions` (string[]) for a service. Pass `null` to either field to clear it. These fields drive the countdown timer and instructions list on the service detail screen.

response.name: 200 OK
400 Bad Request â€“ no fields

request.description: Deletes one service row by id.
Returns all services pinned to the Home > Overview section (`show_on_overview = true`), sorted by `overview_order` ascending.

request.description:
Pins or unpins a single service from the Home > Overview section and/or changes its display order.

Body fields (at least one required):
- `show_on_overview` (boolean): `true` to pin, `false` to unpin
- `overview_order` (number): position in the pinned grid (lower = first)

response.name: 400 Bad Request

request.description:
Sets `overview_order` for multiple services in a single call. Useful for drag-and-drop reordering in an admin UI.

Body:
- `items`: array of `{ id: string, overview_order: number }`

response.name: 400 Bad Request

request.description: Upserts all seed services into Supabase. Useful to restore baseline data.
Returns wallpapers from Supabase ordered by newest first.

Multipart upload endpoint for Android app/admin panel.
- `title`: text
- `image`: file (jpg/png/webp)
Deletes wallpaper from Cloudinary and Supabase.

item.name: Help Content
item.description: CRUD endpoints for the Help tab: FAQs, Dress Code, Do's & Don'ts, and Contact & Support. All data is stored in Supabase. The mobile app reads directly from Supabase (anon key, RLS). These endpoints use the service-role key to write.

request.description: Returns all FAQ rows including inactive ones (admin view).

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Add a new FAQ.

**Required:** `question`, `answer`
**Optional:** `sort_order` (default 0), `is_active` (default true)

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Update any field of an existing FAQ by its numeric `id`.
Permanently delete a FAQ by its numeric `id`.
Returns all dress code rows (including inactive). Ordered by section then sort_order.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Add a new dress code item.

**Required:** `section` (`"men"` | `"women"` | `"general"`), `content`
**Optional:** `sort_order` (default 0), `is_active` (default true)

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Update any field of a dress code item by its numeric `id`.
Permanently delete a dress code item by its numeric `id`.
Returns all do/dont rows (including inactive). Ordered by type then sort_order.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description:
Add a new do or don't item.

**Required:** `type` (`"do"` | `"dont"`), `content`
**Optional:** `sort_order` (default 0), `is_active` (default true)

response.name: 201 Created

request.description: Update any field of a do/don't item by its numeric `id`.
Permanently delete a do/don't item by its numeric `id`.
Returns all contact/support entries (including inactive), ordered by sort_order.

Add a new contact/support entry.

**Required:** `label`, `url`
**Optional:** `sub_label`, `icon` (MaterialCommunityIcons name, default `"web"`), `sort_order`, `is_active`
Update any field of a contact/support entry by its numeric `id`.
Permanently delete a contact/support entry by its numeric `id`.

item.name: SSD Locations
item.description: CRUD for physical SSD token counter locations shown in the mobile app. Frontend reads active-only rows directly via Supabase anon key; these endpoints are for admin management.

request.description: Returns all SSD counter locations ordered by sort_order (includes inactive rows).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "New Counter",
  "area": "Tirumala, Near Temple",
  "timings": "06:00 AM – 06:00 PM",
  "note": "Optional note for pilgrims.",
  "image_url": null,
  "maps_url": "https://maps.google.com/?q=Tirumala+Temple",
  "tag": "New",
  "sort_order": 10,
  "is_active": true
}

request.description: Create a new SSD counter location. Required: name, area, timings, maps_url.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Updated Counter Name",
  "timings": "05:30 AM – 06:30 PM",
  "note": "Updated note.",
  "tag": "Updated",
  "is_active": true
}

request.description: Update any subset of fields for an SSD counter location by its numeric id.
Permanently delete an SSD counter location by its numeric id.

formdata.description: Image file to upload (JPEG/PNG, max 10 MB)

request.description: Upload an image for the location. Uploads to Cloudinary and saves the secure_url + public_id back to Supabase. Replaces any existing image automatically.
Remove the image for a location â€” deletes from Cloudinary and nulls image_url + image_public_id in Supabase.
